warnings: sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose:
Method–provenance block
Property: atom-condensed Fukui functions (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) and dual descriptor for nitrobenzene; headline figure is the largest electrophilic Fukui index f⁺ = 0.49641779 (on C4, atom index 3).
Level of theory: DFT, tier ‘standard’ ≡ B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fitted (RI-JK) in PySCF.
Geometry provenance: single-point analysis on the supplied fixed geometry (/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/nitrobenzene/nitrobenzene.xyz); not re-optimized (denoted Fukui//input).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (implicit continuum off).
Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet (0, 1) base state; cation (+1, 2) and anion (−1, 2) states for N ± 1 densities.
Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill → PySCF backend (version bundled with chemkit 2026-07-02 build).
Convergence & caveats: SCF cycles for all three charge states reported as converged. Integrity checker flags (verbatim in warnings above) note charge-conservation checks; sums equal 1.0000, so no significant drift detected.
Live log: calculation streamed to a timestamped .out file in the working directory while running; it contains full PySCF SCF and Mulliken charge details. Ask if you’d like the path or the full JSON.

Result highlights
• Most electrophilic site: C4 (atom 3, ring carbon ipso to NO₂), f⁺ = 0.49641779.
• Most nucleophilic site: C5 (atom 4, adjacent ring carbon), f⁻ = 0.10728820.
Full per-atom f⁺/f⁻/f⁰ and dual descriptor arrays, as well as a visualization PNG, are supplied in the output directory specified in the method block.
